description_long: This pitchfork features a long, straight wooden handle with a natural, slightly weathered finish, suggesting frequent use. At the top, it has a metal head with three robust, evenly spaced tines that curve slightly outward, designed for efficient lifting and turning of materials. The metal tines are attached to a rounded metal hub, which is securely fastened to the handle. The metal components show some signs of oxidation and wear, giving the tool an authentic, utilitarian appearance. The handle is uncoated and ends in a simple, bare finish without any additional grip or cap. This tool is typical for use in urban gardens, community compost sites, or maintenance yards.
description: A three-tined pitchfork with a long wooden handle and slightly worn metal tines, showing signs of use and oxidation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three evenly spaced, outward-curving metal tines and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the cylindrical wooden handle and the attachment point of the metal head.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the alignment of the tines and the rounded metal hub connecting them to the handle.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, again showing the handle's length and the curvature of the tines.
